title: Frontend Developer | SEB, Solna
description:
Do you want to help shape SEB’s digital Savings experience with modern interfaces and create user-friendly solutions for our customers?
   

  

 SEB is more than just a bank with an IT function. We are a progressive tech company powered by innovation and fuelled by collaboration. From COBOL to AI and everything in between, we offer you an impressive tech stack and several technology paths. Together we're innovating for a sustainable society and building the tech arena of the future, a welcoming space for everyone. Are you ready to take on a new challenge? If so, you'll be interested in this opportunity!
   

  

**About The Role**
 We are responsible for developing and maintaining savings functionality in SEB’s digital channels.
   

  

 This is a role where frontend development is the primary focus - but where understanding backend and DevOps are essentials to succeed. You will work closely with frontend and backend developers, product owners, and designers, contributing to the development of modern, scalable, and maintainable user interfaces.
   

  

 We are looking for someone who is eager to learn, takes initiative, and wants to grow together with us as we continue improving how we build digital products.
   

  

**To thrive in this role, we believe you:**
* have experience with frontend development, preferably with React and TypeScript
* have a good understanding of backend services (.NET or similar) and how frontend and backend work together
* care about writing clean, structured, and maintainable code
* are proactive, take ownership and drive improvements
* enjoy collaborating in cross-functional teams
* want to build digital products that create real customer value
* have a university degree of at least 3 years in Computer Science, Systems Development, or a related field


**Our technical environment:**
**Frontend**
* React / Angular
* TypeScript
* HTML / SCSS


**Backend**
* .NET Core
* C#


**DevOps & Tooling**
* GitHub Actions
* Openshift


**What we offer:**
* A brilliant team of business developers, designers and programmers working closely together on all challenges.
* A flat hierarchy and openness to share ideas, opinions and points of views paired with a strong respect for knowledge and experience.
* Remote working possibilities and super nice offices for close collaboration.


**Do you want to be a part of SEB?**
 It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity is crucial for our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected, and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe or where they come from.
   

  

 Since we select candidates continuously, feel free to send in your application today via the link in this ad, but no later than 22 June. If you have questions about the position, please contact Anton Hänni Talent Acquisition Partner at anton.hanni@seb.se, but we will not process applications via email.
   

  

 Please be aware that our final candidates undergo background checks, a process that includes for example identity control, verification of qualifications, credit checks, company engagements and history of crime. In some cases, we also apply random drug checks.
   

  

 SEB Sweden has a redeployment responsibility, why this position might be covered by internal redeployment
   

  

 Learn more about working at SEB on our Career website or through our Career podcasts.
   

  

 https://sebgroup.com/career
   

  

 https://bit.ly/SEBcareer

title: Principal Software Engineer
description:
Are you driven by innovation and looking to thrive in a fast-paced, growing environment? Join us at Calabrio-Verint and be part of our dynamic team! Help us in reshaping the landscape of customer experience - where every interaction becomes an opportunity, and every insight drives meaningful change.
   

  

 Calabrio-Verint are he trailblazers in customer experience intelligence! Revolutionizing the way organizations connect with their customers, we empower businesses to elevate every interaction to new heights. Our cutting-edge cloud platform, coupled with AI-driven analytics tools, unlocks the true essence of customer sentiment, turning data into actionable insights with lightning speed.
   

  

 As a Principal Software Engineer you are part of an innovative and collaborative team, driving end-to-end delivery of new functionality—from architecture and implementation to deployment for customers—partnering closely with product owners, leads, and agile coaches. Brings deep experience designing and scaling complex systems, leading cross-functional teams, and improving performance, reliability, and user experience across cloud, full-stack, APIs, and DevOps automation. Known for hands-on leadership, mentoring engineers, and aligning technical execution with business strategy.
   

  

 \*\*Candidate must be located in Stockholm, Sweden.\*\*
   

  

**What You'll be doing:**
* Build high-quality web and cloud applications together with a team of dedicated developers
* Work closely with the product owners and customers to turn their ideas and requirements to top-class solutions
* Provide architectural leadership and guidance on complex technical initiatives, ensuring solutions are scalable, secure, maintainable, and aligned with business objectives.
* Foster a collaborative engineering culture through pair programming, mob programming, knowledge sharing, and mentorship.
* Drive continuous improvement of products, platforms, development practices, and operational processes.
* Stay current with emerging technologies, industry trends, and engineering practices, evaluating opportunities to improve products and development efficiency.
* Drive and take initiatives to always improve Verint's products and processes


**Requirements**
* Expert-level software engineering experience with modern backend technologies (C#, Java, or equivalent) and modern frontend frameworks such as React
* Excellent knowledge of web technologies such as HTML and JavaScript libraries
* Thorough experience of test-driven development
* Thorough experience of pair-and/or mob programming
* Thorough experience of cloud platforms such as Azure and AWS
* Thorough experience of agile development methodologies
* Excellent knowledge of database programming fundamentals
* Own technical strategy and architectural direction across multiple teams and products, ensuring alignment with business objectives and long-term platform evolution
* Lead and help others within/outside of your own team, for multiple products
* Develop, follow and share best practices
* Solve complex problems with no standard solutions
* Excellent troubleshooting and analytical skills
* You have a drive to always deliver value to customers
* You proactively keep yourself up to date with the latest trends


**Benefits**
 Calabrio People are: Open, Clear, Ambitious, Accountable, Collaborative, Consistent
   

  

 What we value most...workplace diversity and ensuring an environment of mutual respect.  We believe that diversity and inclusion are critical to our success, and we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Our commitment is to continue to keep our people healthy, focused, and inspire creativity.  Our team members are offered comprehensive benefits for various life circumstances and needs, great opportunities for career development, and a balanced work-life to achieve personal and professional success (all benefits are subject to eligibility requirements). As an Innovator with Purpose, you'll feel motivated and genuinely excited to come to work!
   

  

 Calabrio celebrates and fosters a culture that thrives on diversity. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer that prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind. We provide employees with a work environment free of discrimination and harassment. All employment decisions at Calabrio are based on business needs, job requirements, and individual qualifications, without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran or disability status, parental status, or any other status protected by the laws or regulations in the locations where we operate. We celebrate the >40 nationalities of team members that contribute to our success.
   

  

 We recognize diversity comes in many forms, to foster an inclusive hiring experience any applicants who qualify under the Americans with Disabilities Act, as amended, or applicable state law, who are unable to comply with Calabrio's application process due to their disability may be eligible for a reasonable accommodation. Request for accommodation in the application process can be made by emailing recruiting3@calabrio.com. An applicant requesting accommodation may be required to provide support for the requested accommodation. Calabrio will only share information concerning an applicant's requested accommodation with those individuals who have a specific need to know such information.
   

  

 Ready for Exponential Career Opportunities? Apply now

title: Software Engineer
description:
**ROLE:** 
 Forward Deployed Software Engineer
 



**LOCATION:** 
 Stockholm, Sweden + Operational Deployments
 



**COMPENSATION:** 
 Market Leading Salary + Equity
 



  





 TECHNE is supporting a leading defence technology startup in the search for a Forward Deployed Software Engineer to join their rapidly growing defence technology team, helping deploy sovereign European air defence systems operating in active operational environments globally.
 



  





 They are building next generation counter UAS interceptor platform designed to protect allied skies, borders, and infrastructure through high performance autonomous air defence systems.
 



  





 Their technology combines advanced software, radar integrations, autonomous guidance systems, and modern defence engineering to address one of Europe’s most urgent operational challenges: low cost drone warfare.
 



  





 This role sits at the intersection of software engineering, field integration, defence technology, radar systems, and customer deployment. You will become the first Forward Deployed Software Engineer within the business, helping define how they integrate with customer systems across operational deployments.
 



  





 You will work directly with engineering leadership, customers, and field teams to deploy integrations that operate in real world defence environments, including active deployments in Ukraine.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities:** 



* Lead customer side software integrations across radar and interceptor systems
* Develop production grade Python and C++ integrations against radar APIs and operational platforms
* Deploy into customer environments to support live integrations and operational deployment activities
* Own integrations end to end, from rapid field deployment through scalable production architecture
* Bridge field engineering activities with Stockholm based software and platform teams
* Support productionisation of customer integrations into reusable deployment frameworks
* Help define the Forward Deployed Engineering function, processes, and future hiring roadmap
* Collaborate across engineering, operational, and executive stakeholders within high tempo defence programmes



  





**Required Experience:** 



* Strong production software engineering experience using Python and/or C++
* Experience integrating hardware systems, sensors, APIs, or distributed operational systems
* Comfortable operating within fast paced, customer facing engineering environments
* Strong communication skills across technical and non technical stakeholder groups
* Ability to move between rapid deployment work and long term scalable architecture development
* Experience working within complex or mission critical engineering environments
* Willingness to travel and work onsite.



  





**Desirable Experience:** 



* Forward Deployed Engineering or Solutions Engineering backgrounds
* Experience from companies such as Palantir, Anduril, OpenAI, Anthropic, or similar high performance environments
* Radar, RF systems, MAVLink, ROS/ROS2, autopilot, or sensor fusion exposure
* Rust development experience alongside Python or C++
* Experience integrating classified, defence, or security sensitive systems
* Trading systems or other production software environments operating under high pressure
* Eligibility for NATO aligned security clearance processes



  





**The Why?** 



* Opportunity to become the first Forward Deployed Software Engineer within a rapidly scaling European defence technology company
* Direct operational impact within real world counter drone deployments
* Exposure to live field integration activities supporting allied defence programmes
* Opportunity to define the long term customer engineering and deployment models
* High ownership role operating across engineering, operational deployment, and customer strategy



  





 This is not a traditional software engineering role. It is an opportunity to deploy mission critical software systems operating at the forefront of modern European defence technology.
 



  





 If you would like to discuss the opportunity confidentially, please get in touch directly.

title: Software Engineer/ Senior Frontend Engineer
description:
We are looking for a senior frontend engineer with deep experience in payment flows and enterprise applications. In this role, you will take full ownership of complex features from design to production, set the technical standard for the team, and help build a scalable and secure frontend platform in one of the most demanding domains in digital commerce.
 



  





 You will work in a modern stack with Next.js, React and TypeScript, lead architectural decisions and act as a natural mentor for other engineers. You are comfortable stepping into the BFF layer or Node.js services when needed, and you understand that robust frontend in payment environments requires as much security thinking as technical skill.
 



  





**Responsibilities** 



* Own end-to-end delivery of complex frontend features from requirements to production
* Design scalable, maintainable architecture (modularisation, code splitting, performance)
* Lead state management strategy (Redux, Zustand, React Query) and ensure predictable data flow
* Build and integrate with APIs and BFF layers; contribute to Node.js services where needed
* Deliver accessible, responsive UIs (WCAG-compliant, semantic HTML, CSS)
* Drive performance optimisation, rendering, bundle size, network usage, caching
* Establish and enforce coding standards and best practices across the codebase
* Conduct code reviews and mentor other engineers
* Embed security best practices into development, particularly around payment flows and authentication
* Contribute to CI/CD pipelines and testing strategies (unit, integration, e2e)
* Actively participate in Agile ceremonies and deliver iteratively



  





**Qualifications** 



* Strong experience in the payments domain, particularly checkout flows
* Deep expertise in Next.js, React and TypeScript
* Experience with backend development or debugging against backend services
* Strong communication skills in English, written and spoken
* Self-driven with the ability to lead technical initiatives and raise the bar for the team



  





**Nice to have** 



* Experience with large-scale enterprise applications
* Familiarity with observability tools and structured production debugging
* Experience with security work in regulated environments (PCI-DSS, authentication flows, data protection)



  





**Location** 




 Stockholm preferred
 



  





**About Astek Sweden** 




 Astek has always been driven by a strong curiosity and a passion for the possibilities of technology. This fascination is the foundation of everything we do — our commitment to innovation and product development, the long-term client relationships we build, and the value we create in every assignment, every day. With us, you get the opportunity to grow alongside some of the most skilled engineers and technical experts in the industry.
 



  





**What we offer** 




 We are a company where innovation, community and development are at the heart of what we do. With us, you get the opportunity to work on exciting and challenging projects in an environment where your skills and ideas are valued. We offer:
 


* An inspiring workplace where technology and creativity meet.
* The opportunity to work with leading players in the industry.
* Competence development and career opportunities.
* A company culture where we have fun together and support each other!
* Great benefits including SEK 5,000 in wellness allowance.



  





 We encourage all engineers to apply regardless of gender, background or life situation.
 



  





 Want to be part of our team? You are warmly welcome to submit your application.
 



 We recruit on a rolling basis. Don't hesitate with your application and feel free to contact us if you have any questions.

title: Software Engineer
description:
**Senior C++ Software Engineer / Autonomous Systems, C-UAS, Algorithm / Stockholm (Hybrid) /** 




  





**ROLE:** 
 Senior C++ Software Engineer – Autonomous Systems
 



**LOCATION:** 
 Stockholm, Sweden
 



**COMPENSATION:** 
 Highly Competitive + Equity
 



  





 TECHNE is supporting an advanced autonomous systems company in the search for a Senior C++ Software Engineer to join their growing autonomy engineering team in Stockholm.
 



  





 This role sits at the core of a next-generation autonomous platform, focused on developing the intelligence layer behind a fully in-house autopilot system combining guidance, control, filtering, and perception. These are not theoretical systems. The technology is progressing toward real-world deployment and operational use.
 



  





 You will work on embedded autonomy software responsible for how the system behaves in live operating conditions, translating advanced control and perception concepts into robust real-time software solutions.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities:**



* Develop embedded C++ software for autonomous systems
* Design and implement guidance, navigation, and control logic
* Develop filtering and sensor fusion systems for real-time decision making
* Integrate perception inputs from visible and thermal camera systems
* Deploy real-time software onto MCU platforms, including STM32
* Work within RTOS environments such as FreeRTOS
* Support integration between software, hardware, and system-level behaviour
* Contribute to the autonomy stack architecture and system evolution
* Collaborate closely with embedded software, systems, and autonomy engineers
* Produce technical documentation covering software performance and validation



  





**Required Experience:**



* Degree in Robotics, Aerospace Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics, or related STEM discipline
* Strong modern C++ development experience
* Experience developing real-time embedded software systems
* Understanding of guidance, control, filtering, or sensor fusion techniques
* Experience working on embedded MCU platforms
* Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities



  





**Desirable Experience:**



* Autonomous systems or autopilot development
* Sensor fusion and perception systems
* RTOS environments including FreeRTOS
* STM32 or similar embedded platforms
* Computer vision or thermal imaging integration
* Real-time control systems
* Embedded Linux environments
* Defence, aerospace, robotics, or UAV experience



  





 This is an opportunity to join a high-performing engineering team building advanced autonomous systems with direct impact on the future of European defence technology.
 



  





 The position offers strong compensation, meaningful stock options, and the opportunity to shape core autonomy technology from the ground up.
 



  





 If you would like to discuss the position confidentially, please get in touch directly.

title: Frontend Developer – Planning
description:
10% of Europe’s CO₂ emissions are caused by residential heating, and energy bills are out of control. Aira is here to change that.
   

  

 With our intelligent Aira Heat Pump at the heart, we’re making the switch to clean energy-tech easier and more affordable. Helping Europe turn off gas and slash energy costs one home at a time.
   

  

 Founded in 2022, and with 1200+ employees and counting, Aira is driving the clean energy revolution by providing homes with modern clean energy-tech solutions such as solar power, battery storage solutions, and heat pumps. With a state-of-the-art production facility in Poland, and HQ in Stockholm, Sweden, and hubs across the UK, Germany, and Italy, Aira is well on track to install clean energy-tech into 5 million homes by 2034.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As a Frontend Developer on our Planning team, you'll help transform how clean energy gets delivered—by empowering our planners with intuitive, data-driven tools. This team is responsible for optimizing the scheduling of Aira’s field force. The domain stretches across the whole customer journey, including tools for booking and coordinating sales visits, heat pump installations, and maintenance. You'll work at the intersection of software and real-world impact, building data products that help planners effectively schedule our field force based on proximity, dates, and other critical factors.
   

  

**In this role, you will:**
* Build modern user interfaces that enable planners to visualize and optimize complex scheduling scenarios—bringing clarity, efficiency, and effectiveness to workforce planning
* Develop interactive map-based tools that help planners make better decisions based on geographical proximity, travel times, and resource availability
* Create intuitive dashboards that collect and visualize operational data, helping planners identify patterns, balance workloads, and make smarter scheduling decisions
* Design user experiences that simplify the planning process and reduce cognitive load—enabling planners to focus on strategic decisions rather than administrative tasks
* Support dynamic rescheduling capabilities with clear interfaces that highlight impacts and alternatives when changes occur
* Help us scale into new regions and markets by building flexible interfaces that adapt to varying local requirements, scheduling constraints, and service models


 We're changing how clean energy visits are planned and scheduled—making it smarter, faster, and better for everyone involved. If you're excited about building modern tooling and UIs that empower planning teams and drive tangible outcomes, this is the role for you.
   

  

**What You’ll Bring To The Role**
* Solid experience with product-driven software development and agile environments
* Experience with React and TypeScript
* Strong visualization skills, especially with maps and timeline interfaces


 We would also expect you to have experience in at least two of the following areas:
   

  

  

* Rapid growth journeys (start-ups, scale-ups, new environments)
* Kanban, Scrum, and/or a combination of both
* Next.js
* Data visualization libraries (D3.js, Chart.js, etc.)
* Map integration (Mapbox, Google Maps, Leaflet)
* Experience building planning or scheduling interfaces


**What is in it for you?**
* Gain hands-on experience in a fast-growing scale-up, with a supportive and inclusive culture, and career advancement opportunities.
* Collaborate with diverse teams across international markets and launch products globally.
* Develop and grow personally and professionally in an entrepreneurial-minded environment.
* Thrive in a dynamic, solution-oriented culture, tackling challenges and driving meaningful solutions that make a positive impact on the world.
* Influence the company's success, with a visible impact on product development and market growth.
* Enjoy the flexibility of our hybrid work model and discover our brand-new office at Norra Stationsgatan 93 in Stockholm. Conveniently located near public transport, with Sankt Eriksplan and Odenplan stations just a few minutes' walk away


 At Aira, we champion the next-generation energy as well as diversity and inclusion. We firmly believe that a diverse workforce fosters innovation and creativity, enhancing our ability to serve customers and communities effectively. We welcome individuals from all backgrounds, cultures, and perspectives to join our team. Our commitment is to uphold equality and inclusivity, fostering a collaborative environment where everyone can thrive and contribute to a better future for our people and the planet.

title: Staff .NET Developer
description:
**What will you do?**
 As a Staff .NET Developer at Rebtel you will serve as the technical guardian and principal individual contributor for the entire Backend (BE) architecture and ecosystem. This role is crucial for ensuring the technical excellence, scalability, reliability, and security of all backend systems that power our core products and services. The Staff Software Engineer will operate at a high level of abstraction, influencing engineering practices across multiple teams while remaining hands-on in critical areas.
   

  

 You’ll be actively involved in architectural decisions, contribute to technical discussions, and inspire your team to deliver clean, robust code.
   

  

**Responsibilities And Tasks**
* Lead the design, development, and deployment of complex, high-throughput backend services and APIs, ensuring best-in-class performance and maintainability.
* Drive architectural decisions and serve as the ultimate technical escalation point for BE-related challenges.
* Establish and articulate a clear, ambitious long-term vision and technical roadmap for the evolution of the BE systems.
* This includes planning for future growth, anticipating technological shifts, and driving the adoption of new technologies and patterns to maintain a competitive edge.
* Act as the primary architect for major system initiatives, defining microservices boundaries, data models, integration patterns, and deployment strategies.
* Proactively identify and resolve technical debt and architectural risks before they impact the business.
* Mentor engineers, elevating the technical expertise and quality of engineering output across the organization.
* Influence engineering standards and best practices for testing, performance tuning, and security across different development teams.


**Requirements**
* Exceptional and proven proficiency in the .NET area, including extensive experience with C#, .NET Core/8+, and related frameworks (e.g., ASP. NET Core, Entity Framework Core).
* Must demonstrate the ability to architect and build high-performance, fault-tolerant systems using the .NET stack.
* Demonstrable architecting skills with a strong background in designing and implementing large-scale, distributed systems (e.g., microservices, event-driven architectures, serverless).
* Expert knowledge of design patterns, domain-driven design (DDD), and SOLID principles.
* Extensive experience with at least one major cloud platform (AWS, Azure, or GCP) and related services (e.g., managed databases, message queues, containerization).
* Deep understanding of various database technologies (SQL and NoSQL) and data modeling best practices for scalable applications.
* System-Level Thinking: Ability to decompose complex problems into manageable components and evaluate trade-offs between different technical solutions (e.g., speed vs. stability, buy vs. build).
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present complex technical ideas clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, driving consensus on critical architectural decisions.


**Why Rebtel?**
 Rebtel has been connecting people across borders for nearly 20 years. Today, we’re profitable, growing, and at a pivotal moment in our journey. As we enter our next phase, we’re building an organisation designed for speed, ownership, and real impact, where every role contributes directly to shaping what comes next.
   

  

 This is a place with global ambition and a strong foundation, where ideas move quickly and decisions matter. You won’t get lost in layers of process or slow-moving structures. Instead, you’ll find the space to take ownership, collaborate across teams, and make meaningful contributions from day one. Based in Stockholm, we bring together a diverse, international team united by a shared purpose: to simplify the way people connect worldwide.
   

  

**Benefits**
 At Rebtel, you are the most important asset and we strive to provide a comprehensive package of benefits and perks that enhance your well-being and work experience. Here are some of the things you can expect from us:
   

  

* Pension Plan
* Health Checkups, Influenza shots and Private Medical Insurance
* Dental Insurance
* Occupational insurance
* Wellness allowance (5,000 SEK)
* Discount on gym memberships
* Bonus program
* Extra parental pay
* 30 days annual vacation
* Monday breakfasts
* Relocation Support, if you're joining us from afar, we'll assist you in making a smooth transition.


*We are Rebtel. We come from all around the world to create products for anyone who has crossed a border. We believe in equal opportunity and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.*
 Rebtel is a company of about 70 people from 31 countries who share a common mission to empower diaspora communities around the world to stay connected to where they come from. From our office in central Stockholm, we help our user base of 1 million dual-culture people—predominantly from the global south—connect with and support their communities through affordable data-free international calling and mobile top-ups. With our app and online platform, we help dissolve the space between their worlds so they can live more connected lives.

title: Full Stack Java Developer to Financial Crime Prevention | SEB, Solna
description:
*SEB is more than just a bank with an IT function. We are a progressive tech company powered by innovation and fueled by collaboration. From Java to AI and everything in between, we offer you an impressive tech stack and several technology paths. Together we're innovating for a sustainable society and building the tech arena of the future, a welcoming space for everyone.*
**About The Role**
 This role is a unique opportunity to shape the future of financial crime prevention through technology. As a core contributor, you will help define new ways of working for FCP Investigators by building intuitive and efficient workflow tools that help them identify and act on suspicious activity faster. Your work will make investigative tools more effective and impactful, supporting SEB’s efforts to protect society from financial crime. You will also contribute to innovation within the FCP Process Automation team, including our GenAI assistant that transforms how intelligence is captured and used. If you are passionate about future-proof technology, process efficiency, and real-world impact, this role is for you.
   

  

 As a Java Developer, you will work with specialists across the team, including Workflow Developers and Data Engineers, to build automated processes and integrate them with company systems. The team is distributed between Solna and Warsaw.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
 Design and build components that connect internal FCP systems with process automation solutions.
   

  

* Work with developers and IT architects to shape process implementation designs.
* Guide business stakeholders on technical options, recommended solutions and key limitations.
* Improve team practices by identifying opportunities, proposing changes and strengthening delivery.


**To thrive in this role, we believe you:**
* Learn quickly and adapt readily to change.
* Appreciate balancing the ideal solution with the need to deliver practical, working outcomes.
* Build strong relationships that support effective collaboration and value delivery.


**Have experience:**
* Developing applications in Java.
* Developing front-end applications, preferably using JavaScript or TypeScript, HTML/CSS and modern frameworks.
* or knowledge of Kafka, MongoDB and container environments such as Docker or OpenShift.
* of cloud services, preferably Google Cloud Platform (GCP).
* of prompt engineering and AI software development.
* of scrum or other agile ways of working.
* Familiarity with business process analysis, workflow automation products, low code platforms or a related field is an advantage.
* Have excellent command of the English language both written and spoken.


**What We Offer**
* We offer many experiences and benefits to our employees, and there is nuance to every individual’s career experience, but the elements that define the core of our offering are:
* Extensive training and learning opportunities.
* Work-life balance.
* International opportunities and working environment.
* Access to SEB staff banking with exclusive benefits.
* Innovative company in forefront of technology.


**Do you want to be part of SEB?**
 Attach your CV and a personal letter describing yourself and how you can contribute to SEB and our team. Since we meet candidates continuously, feel free to send in your application today, but no later than
 **2026-07-31** 
 .
   

  

 It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity is crucial for our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe or where they come from. SEB Sweden has a redeployment responsibility, which is why this position might be covered by internal redeployment.

title: Product Owner and Cloud Developer for the Cloud Tribe | SEB, Solna
description:
**Product Owner and Cloud Developer for the Cloud Tribe at SEB**
*SEB is more than just a bank with a technology function. We are a progressive tech company powered by innovation and fueled by collaboration. From Java to AI and everything in between, we offer you an impressive tech stack and several technology paths. Together we're innovating for a sustainable society and building the tech arena of the future, a welcoming space for everyone. Are you looking for a meaningful challenge and an opportunity to grow? If so, you'll be interested in this opportunity!*
**About The Role**
 As a Product Owner for the Azure & AWS Core team, you will be the product lead of a team of highly engaged peers using cutting-edge technology to develop and maintain foundational services on Azure & AWS. You will be instrumental in ensuring our technical Governance & Compliance, working closely with operations, maintenance, and security of our managed cloud platform.
   

  

 You will play a crucial role in product management, overseeing the creation, maintenance, and updates of Cloud landing zones to meet various needs across SEB Group domains and divisions. Your responsibilities will include defining product vision, strategy, and roadmap, prioritizing features, and ensuring alignment with business goals. You will collaborate with stakeholders to gather requirements, manage product backlogs, and ensure timely delivery of high-quality solutions.
   

  

 Our delivery includes guardrails, best practices, and supporting functionalities, paving the way for SEB’s developers to create a modern bank in the Clouds. Join us in building the foundation of a paradigm shift in banking. As part of our multi-cloud tribe, you will gain skills and experience in multiple Public Cloud environments.
   

  

**To Thrive in This Position, You Should Have:**
* Product Management Skills: Ability to define product vision, strategy, and roadmap, prioritize features, and align with business goals.
* Stakeholder Collaboration: Experience in gathering requirements, managing product backlogs, keeping on top of expectations and ensuring timely delivery.
* Hands-on experience with Azure & AWS: Demonstrated ability to manage and optimize cloud services on these platforms.
* Understanding of other Cloud Platforms such as Google Cloud: Ability to integrate and leverage diverse cloud environments.
* Experience with Infrastructure as Code (e.g., Terraform), automation, and CI/CD: Proficiency in deploying and managing infrastructure efficiently.
* Proficiency in TypeScript, Bash, and Python: Strong coding skills to support automation and development tasks.
* Understanding of logging, monitoring, and observability platforms: Ability to ensure system reliability and performance.
* Knowledge of Agile principles: Experience in Agile methodologies to manage product development cycles.
* Motivation and eagerness to learn: A proactive attitude towards continuous improvement and learning.
* Strong communication and presentation skills in both English and Swedish.


**What We Offer**
* Entrepreneurial Scandinavian Environment: Work-life balance, international working environment, hybrid work model, empowering and welcoming culture.
* Growth Culture: Extensive training and learning opportunities, local and international internal mobility possibilities, long-term stability.
* Modern Environment: Innovative company at the forefront of technology, challenging and cutting-edge work, excellent office environment, agile and modern ways of working.
* Additional Monetary Benefits: Paid seniority vacation and volunteering days, allocations to your pension fund, profit-sharing programme, and additional health insurance.


**Do You Want to Be Part of SEB?**
 Welcome to our inclusive culture, where our shared values inspire and uplift our team. We celebrate diversity and strive to ensure every employee feels seen, heard, and valued.
   

  

 Since we select candidates continuously, feel free to send in your application today via the link in this ad, but no later than
 **June 30th, 2026.**
**Learn more about working at SEB: Technology | SEB**
 Please be aware that our final candidates undergo background checks, a process that includes for example identity control, verification of qualifications, credit checks, company engagements and history of crime. In some cases, we also apply random drug checks. During employment, employees within SEB can also expect to undergo recurring background checks. SEB Sweden has a redeployment responsibility, why this position might be covered by internal redeployment.

title: Senior Platform Engineer
description:
Why Doktor.se
   

  

 Doktor.se is Sweden's largest digital healthcare provider. We combine physical clinics with a digital platform serving millions of consultations per year across Sweden, Germany, and Norway. Our 25-person engineering team has startup autonomy inside a profitable, international organization with 1,000+ employees and over 1B SEK in revenue.
   

  

 We run OpenAI and Anthropic models in production across patient and clinician workflows, and that footprint is growing fast.
   

  

 What you'll do
   

  

 Our infrastructure works, but it was built to solve yesterday's problems. We need someone who can help us get to where we want to be — not maintain what we have, but reshape it. If you want a place where everything is already figured out, this isn't it. If you want to build something better, here's what you'd own:
   

  

* Build a deployment platform where teams ship their own services without asking you for permission.
* Design infrastructure that is secure by default, so teams don't have to think about encryption, network isolation, or data residency. It just works.
* Create deployment pipelines where security scanning, policy checks, and compliance happen automatically, invisible when everything is fine, loud when something needs attention.
* Support and scale infrastructure for AI workloads, we run models in production across our platform, and that footprint is growing.
* Set up observability that gives teams real insight into their own services, useful dashboards, alerts that mean something, not noise that everyone ignores.
* Work closely with developers to understand what slows them down, and then fix it. Not by doing it for them, but by making it easy for them to do it themselves.
* Maintain and evolve our cloud account structure, networking, and identity management across multiple regions and products.


 Some weeks you'll write infrastructure code. Some weeks you'll pair with a developer to debug a slow service. Some weeks you'll design how a new product gets its own isolated infrastructure. The common thread: you make the teams around you better.
   

  

*Make developers faster, not more dependent on you.*
 What we're looking for
   

  

* You've built and operated Kubernetes in production. Not just deployed a Helm chart once, you've debugged networking issues, designed RBAC policies, and thought about how multiple teams share a cluster without stepping on each other.
* You know AWS well. Accounts, networking, IAM, KMS. You have opinions about what should live in Kubernetes and what genuinely needs to be an AWS managed service, and you lean toward keeping things in Kubernetes where possible.
* You've worked with GitOps and infrastructure as code. Flux, ArgoCD, Terrafor, specific tools matter less than the principle: infrastructure changes go through pull requests, are reviewed, and are auditable.
* You understand developers because you are one. You can read their code, understand their pipelines, and have meaningful conversations about architecture.
* You think about security as something you design into systems, not something you review at the end.
* You have opinions, and you can change them when someone makes a better argument.
* University degree in computer science, engineering, or equivalent experience.


 Tech stack
   

  

**Cloud** 
 - AWS (multi-account, multi-region)
   

  

**Orchestration -** 
 Kubernetes / EKS
   

  

**IaC -** 
 Terraform
   

  

**CI/CD -** 
 GitHub Actions
   

  

**Languages -** 
 Python, TypeScript
   

  

**AI -** 
 OpenAI, Anthropic (in production and tooling)
   

  

**Observability -** 
 Datadog / Grafana (you'll shape this)
   

  

 What we offer
   

  

* Real influence. You'll shape how our platform works, not just operate what someone else designed.
* Healthcare context. Patient data, regulatory compliance, and real consequences for getting it wrong. What you build actually matters.
* Small team, direct impact. 25 engineers, your work is visible, not lost in a machine.
* Autonomy. No micromanagement, no endless approval chains.
* AI in production. We're running LLMs in patient and clinician workflows today, the infrastructure needs to grow with that.
* Collective agreement, 30 days vacation, wellness allowance, personal development budget.


 Practical details
   

  

* Start: As soon as we find the right person. We'll accommodate your notice period.
* Employment: Permanent, full-time. Six month probation period.
* Location: On-site, Monday–Thursday at Sveavägen 63, Stockholm. Fridays are flexible.
* Language: English is our working language in tech. Swedish is a bonus.
* Work permit: You must already have the right to work in Sweden.
* Contact: Andrew Crookston, VP of Engineering


 We handle recruitment internally — no agencies, no consultants, no external recruiters.
   

  

 Doktor.se was founded in 2016 and is one of Sweden’s biggest providers of digital healthcare and digital operations across the world.
   

  

 Our ambition is to improve healthcare worldwide and make it more efficient. By making the first contact digital, we save time and resources for both the individual and our society at large. More patients receive care faster, while traditional healthcare is relieved. This frees up resources for patients who need physical appointments.

title: Forward Deployed Engineer | Saab
description:
*Want to influence the bigger picture? At Saab, you can realize amazing innovations that keep people and societies safe. Together, we can truly make an impact – and your part matters.* 









*Common Tech is the engine behind Saab’s technological transformation—building the shared platforms, capabilities, and foundations that enable the entire organization to scale, innovate, and deliver at speed.* 









*Within this ecosystem, Product Tech plays a unique role—bridging the gap between technology and real-world impact. It ensures that the capabilities built in Common Tech are not just available—but adopted, integrated, and used where it matters most: in real products, by real teams. A particular focus lies on AI-based solutions that will be integrated into Saab's product portfolio and used broadly across the organization. What you help create in Product Tech has direct impact across multiple products, teams and business areas. Product Tech is where Saab’s future technology is shaped—and where it is brought into real use across the whole organization.* 




  





**Do you want to help bridge the gap between today and the future - and play a key role in driving Saab's digital transformation?** 




 This is not a traditional developer role. It's a role for curious minds who enjoy variety, creativity and deep technical thinking.
 



  





**Your role** 




 We are now looking for a Forward Deployed Engineer to join Product Tech at Saab. You will play a key role in both strategic important digitalization initiatives and Forward Deployed engagements, helping accelerate innovation and enable real impact across the organization.
 



  





 This role sits at the heart of Saab's digital strategy execution model, where different types of engagements come together:
 


* Strategic, business-critical initiatives focused on developing new solutions and pushing the boundaries of current capabilities
* High-impact efforts where you enable teams adopt and implement modern technologies, platforms, and ways of working in real product environments



 As a Forward Deployed Engineer you will operate across both - building new capabilities while also ensuring they are successfully deployed, adopted, and used in practice.
 



  





**What you'll do** 



* Work hands-on with complex technical problems across different product areas
* Build and improve shared platforms and components used throughout Saab
* Explore and apply technologies like AI, cloud, edge computing and real-time systems
* Collaborate with engineers, architects and domain experts
* Turn ambiguous challenges into clear, elegant solutions
* Influence technical direction and long-term platform strategy



  





**Your profile** 




 We believe you thrive in environments where you balance hands-on development with enabling others to succeed. You enjoy building new solutions, improving existing platforms, and helping teams move forward in situations where requirements evolve and ambiguity is part of the journey.
 



  





 You bring a strong software engineering background combined with a pragmatic, solution-oriented mindset. You are comfortable navigating complex and fast-moving environments, bringing structure, clarity, and technical direction when needed. At the same time, you enjoy collaborating with others, sharing knowledge, and supporting teams through coaching and technical leadership while remaining hands-on.
 



  





**Experience** 



* Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field, or equivalent work experience
* Minimum 2 years of experience in software engineering/development, preferably within complex systems or product environments
* Proven experience working with cloud-based solutions, CI/CD pipelines, and modern software development practices, system design and architecture, preferably in modular or distributed systems
* Experience and/or understanding of how AI and data can be applied to create real-world value in products, beyond experimentation or prototyping



  





**Technical competence** 



* Strong capability to design, build, and integrate solutions within cloud, data, and/or AI domains
* Experience working with modular architecture and reusable building blocks
* Ability to develop robust, scalable, and reusable solutions that can be leveraged across teams and products
* Understanding of how to design future-proof systems that support long-term evolution and reuse



  





**Personal qualities** 



* Curious and eager to explore new technologies, tools, and ways of working
* Collaborative mindset with strong communication and coaching skills
* Comfortable working in environments where priorities and requirements evolve over time
* Ability to create structure, clarity, and direction in complex environments
* Motivated by enabling both technology and people to grow



  





**Bonus if you have** 



* Experience from real-time or safety-critical environments



  





*Some of our roles require security clearance under Swedish security protection legislation. For roles where Saab has requirements for security classification, this may in some cases entail requirements for a certain citizenship.* 




  





**What you become part of** 




**Common Tech** 
 is the engine behind Saab’s technological transformation—building the shared platforms, capabilities, and foundations that enable the entire organization to scale, innovate, and deliver at speed.
 



  





 Within this ecosystem,
 **Product Tech** 
 plays a unique role—bridging the gap between technology and real-world impact. It ensures that the capabilities built in Common Tech are not just available—but adopted, integrated, and used where it matters most: in real products, by real teams. A particular focus lies on AI-based solutions that will be integrated into Saab's product portfolio and used broadly across the organization. What you help create in Product Tech has direct impact across multiple products, teams and business areas. Product Tech is where Saab’s future technology is shaped—and where it is brought into real use across the whole organization.
 



  





**Saab** 
 is a leading defence and security company with a clear mission: to help protect people and societies. With around 20,000 employees worldwide, Saab develops advanced technology through close collaboration between people, competencies and disciplines. The company is characterised by an inclusive culture where collaboration, learning and long-term development are central.
 



  





*These positions require approved security clearance under Swedish security protection legislation.* 




  





**Application** 




 This recruitment process is managed by Jefferson Wells on behalf of Saab, meaning you will be directly employed by Saab. For questions about the position, please contact recruiter Mark Ferrer or Sara Takahashi.
 



  





 We do not accept applications by email due to GDPR.
 



  





**Selection and interviews are ongoing. We warmly welcome your application!**

title: Senior Android Developer

We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.*
 Rebtel is a company of about 70 people from 31 countries who share a common mission to empower diaspora communities around the world to stay connected to where they come from. From our office in central Stockholm, we help our user base of 1 million dual-culture people—predominantly from the global south—connect with and support their communities through affordable data-free international calling and mobile top-ups.
