title: Prognostic Engineer
description:
Transport is at the core of modern society. Imagine using your expertise to shape sustainable transport and infrastructure solutions for the future. If you seek to make a difference on a global scale, working with next-gen technologies and the sharpest collaborative teams, then we could be a perfect match.

 **What you will do**


In this role, you will help develop data-driven prognostics solutions that improve vehicle uptime and support smarter maintenance decisions.

  



You will work with diagnostic signals, traces, and vehicle data to understand how systems degrade over time, identify trends, and define what should be predicted, for whom, and over what horizon.

  



You will translate complex technical and operational needs into clear analytical requirements, helping shape solutions that can predict remaining useful life and support more tailored preventive maintenance.

  



Working closely with data scientists, software developers, component experts, and service engineers, you will help validate outputs, assess business value, and turn insights into practical digital products and service tools for dealers, technicians, fleet managers, and operators.

  

You will also contribute to how data is structured, stored, and used, making sure the solutions are grounded in engineering reality and can be deployed effectively in a cross-functional product development environment.

 **Your future team**


You will join the Technology & Service Development team (TSD), where the mission is to keep customers’ vehicles on the road by maximizing uptime through smart, data-driven maintenance solutions. In the prognostics space, the team builds end-to-end solutions from signal processing and health modeling to decision support and service integration.

  



**Who are you?**


In this role, you will bring a solution-oriented mindset and a genuine interest in failure physics and data science, combined with strong ownership of digital products. You are an applied analytics/engineering professional who combines analytical capability with sound engineering judgment, business awareness and a clear customer focus.

  

You thrive in cross-functional collaboration and are comfortable navigating uncertainty when degradation is complex and data is incomplete. You build trustful relationships that strengthen both delivery and team culture.

  

Prognostic Engineer — an engineering and data product role grounded in strong data science understanding, requiring:

* An MSc in Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Vehicle Engineering, or equivalent.
* 5+ years of experience in engineering or applied analytics role
* Solid understanding of vehicle systems/technologies, diagnostics, and how system degradations show up in data.
* Ability to read, interpret, and challenge analytical outputs, you don’t need to build the models, but you need to understand what they’re telling you and when to trust them.
* Experience working in cross-functional, agile product development environments.
* Knowledge in OBD/CAN data, ECU log analysis and telematics data architecture.
  

Nice to have:

* Familiarity with RUL estimation frameworks, survival analysis, or health index approaches.
* Exposure to cloud or big data platforms (Azure, Databricks) from an engineering perspective.
* Knowledge of aftermarket service operations, uptime contracts, or maintenance planning workflows.
 **What’s in it for you?**

* Being part of a mission-driven team focused on maximizing customer uptime through smart maintenance solutions
* Close collaboration across engineering, data science and software, with strong product ownership and real-world impact
* Opportunities to grow your expertise at the intersection of vehicle systems, analytics and service planning
 **Ready for the next move?**


If you are genuinely curious about how vehicles fail and want to build a career at the intersection of engineering and data-driven maintenance — we want to hear from you.

  

If you have any questions about the position, please contact:


Jörgen Ivarsson, Group Manager Prognostics Gothenburg.

  

Last application date: 28:th of June


We value your data privacy and therefore do not accept applications via mail.

 **Who we are and what we believe in**  

We are committed to shaping the future landscape of efficient, safe, and sustainable transport solutions. Fulfilling our mission creates countless career opportunities for talents across the group’s leading brands and entities.


Applying to this job offers you the opportunity to join **Volvo Group**. Every day, you will be working with some of the sharpest and most creative brains in our field to be able to leave our society in better shape for the next generation. We are passionate about what we do, and we thrive on teamwork. We are almost 100,000 people united around the world by a culture of care, inclusiveness, and empowerment.

 **Trucks Technology & Industrial Division** hire team players who are ready to create real customer impact. Our decentralized teams work close to our customers, with speed and autonomy, to build what they truly need.  

Join us to collaborate on innovative, sustainable technologies that redefine how we design, build, and deliver value. Bring your curiosity, your expertise, and your collaborative energy, and together, we’ll turn bold ideas into tangible solutions for our customers and contribute to a more sustainable tomorrow.
company_description: Every day, we work with some of the sharpest and most creative brains in our field to leave our society in better shape for the next generation.

title: Cloud Account Executive
description:
**Description**
 About Us
   

  

 Tableau helps people see and understand data. As the world's leading analytics platform, we empower everyone from global enterprises to early-stage startups to transform their data into actionable insights. We are passionate about our mission and product, and we value work-life balance, efficiency, and making a difference. As part of Salesforce, we are dedicated to driving customer success through innovation.
   

  

 Your Impact
   

  

 You will be the dedicated sales professional for strategic enterprise clients in
 **Sweden** 
 . Your mission is to forge deep, strategic partnerships with leading Swedish enterprises across key industries including manufacturing, retail, automotive, and financial services. You will drive digital transformation by embedding Analytics solutions across their business operations, helping them unlock the power of data in everything from supply chain optimization and customer analytics to operational efficiency and strategic decision-making. This is a high-visibility role focused on strategic growth, executive engagement, and long-term customer success in one of Europe's most digitally mature and innovative markets.
   

  

 What You'll Be Doing
   

  

* Strategic Account Management: Develop and execute comprehensive account plans for designated enterprise accounts in Sweden, aligning Tableau and Analytics solutions with their core business objectives to meet and exceed sales quotas.
* Executive Relationship Building: Build and maintain strong, C-level relationships across your clients' organizations, acting as a trusted advisor on their data and analytics journey within the Swedish and Nordic business context.
* Full-Cycle Sales: Drive the entire sales cycle from opportunity identification to closure, expertly managing a complex pipeline and navigating large enterprise procurement processes.
* The Team Player: You know that "winning" is a team sport. You excel at engaging internal resources and partners to close complex, multi-million dollar deals.
* Pipeline & Forecasting: Meticulously manage and track all customer and transactional information in Salesforce (CRM), providing accurate and regular reporting of your pipeline and forecast.
* Market Expertise: Serve as an expert on the Swedish enterprise landscape and competitive environment, staying current with local market trends, and effectively communicating Tableau's value proposition.


**Who You Are (Required Skills & Experience)**
* Languages: Professional fluency in English is essential. Fluency or professional proficiency in Swedish is highly preferred to effectively navigate the local market and build deep client relationships.
* Industry Experience: You have 8-10+ years of enterprise software sales experience, with significant experience selling into the Swedish enterprise market across diverse industry verticals.
* Market Expertise: You possess a deep understanding of the Swedish business culture, flat organizational hierarchies, and consensus-driven decision-making processes, with a proven track record of successfully selling to large multinational corporations and domestic enterprises.
* Proven Performer: You have a history of consistently overachieving sales quotas and experience managing complex deals in Sweden and the wider Nordic region.
* Solution Seller: You are an expert in value-based or solution selling of enterprise SaaS platforms (e.g., BI, data analytics, CRM, ERP).
* Go-Getter: You are self-directed, resourceful, and have a strong work ethic. You are driven to build strategic relationships and maximize account revenue.
* Excellent Communicator: You have outstanding presentation and communication skills, with the ability to articulate complex concepts clearly and persuasively to both technical and business audiences in Swedish and English.


 Unleash Your Potential
   

  

 When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and be your best, and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can do your best. Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world.
   

  

 At Salesforce, we strive to create an accessible and inclusive experience for all candidates.
   

  

 If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodation Request Form.
   

  

 Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options.
   

  

 Equal Opportunity Statement.
   

  

 Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.
   

  

**Salesforce welcomes all.**
 In the United States, compensation offered will be determined by factors such as location, job level, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Salesforce offers a variety of benefits to help you live well including: time off programs, medical, dental, vision, mental health support, paid parental leave, life and disability insurance, 401(k), and an employee stock purchasing program. More details about company benefits can be found at the following link: https://www.salesforcebenefits.com.

title: Senior IAM Engineer | SEB, Solna
description:
The IAM Platform team is now looking for a new team member. We work closely together and share the passion for great technology and creating a smooth engineering experience. Our group consists of people with different expertise, experiences, age and background. We have an open and friendly approach towards each other.
   

  

**About the role:**
 At this position within Integrations & Connectivity, you will join a highly skilled IAM team responsible for designing, building, and operating SEB’s Identity and Access Management platforms across both on-prem and cloud environments. The team works with modern IAM technologies, strong security practices, and deeply integrated solutions that are part of SEB’s DevOps toolchains and digital platforms.
   

  

 As a Senior IAM Engineer, you will take an active role in evolving our IAM platforms by analyzing needs, designing architecture, and implementing robust technical solutions. IAM is a highly prioritized area at SEB, which means close collaboration with development teams, security, cloud, and infrastructure units across the organization. You will also be a key contributor to SEB’s cloud journey, ensuring that IAM capabilities support scalable, secure, and compliant cloud services.
   

  

**You will be responsible for:**
* Designing and implementing IAM solutions and architectures
* Operating and evolving IAM platforms and infrastructure
* Supporting cloud and platform onboarding with secure IAM patterns
* Troubleshooting identity related issues and ensuring compliance with security policies
* Continuously improving the organization’s overall security posture in close collaboration with cross- functional teams


**To thrive in this role, we believe you have:**
* 5 years or more experience within IAM engineering. Expert and hands on experience on access management tools
* Good knowledge of networking fundamentals (TLS, DNS, proxies, firewalls, VPNs)
* Advanced knowledge of certificate management and PKI, including handling SSL/TLS certificates
* Advanced knowledge with identity federation technology (ex. OpenID Connect, OAuth2).
* Experience on managing different IDP solutions (Identity providers)
* Good knowledge with Single Sign-On and Multi Factor Authentication solution (ex. FIDO2).
* Advanced knowledge with DevSecOps tools (e.g. managing platforms with Kubernetes, Docker, Git, Github actions).
* Advanced knowledge in programming languages, such as Java, Python, Go, Spring or Javascript.
* Good knowledge in Linux, Idap, Openshift and Infra-as-code (Terraform, Ansible and Helm)
* Experience working in hybrid and multi-cloud enterprise environments, including secure integration across multiple cloud providers and on-premises platforms.
* Hands-on experience with one or more enterprise identity platforms, such as Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD), Keycloak, or Curity.
* Experience operating within complex and highly integrated IT environments.
* Advanced troubleshooting capabilities and a deep understanding of IT infrastructure with the ability to diagnose and resolve complex identity and access issues across diverse systems and environments.


**What we offer:**
* An international and empowering environment with highly skilled colleagues.
* Work-life balance & hybrid way of working.
* Extensive training and learning opportunities.
* Friendly and welcoming culture.
* Exciting opportunity to be part of transforming an industry.
* Attractive compensation and benefits.


 Do you want to be part of SEB?
   

  

 It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity is crucial to our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe or where they come from.
   

  

 Since we select candidates continuously, feel free to send in your application today.. If you have questions about the position, please contact our talent acquisition partner louise.almberg@seb.se Please not that we will not process applications via email.
   

  

 Please be aware that our final candidates undergo background checks, a process that includes for example identity control, verification of qualifications, credit checks, company engagements and history of crime. In some cases, we also apply random drug checks. During employment, employees within SEB can also expect to undergo recurring background checks.
   

  

 SEB Sweden has a redeployment responsibility, why this position might be covered by internal redeployment
   

  

 Learn more about working at SEB here: sebgroup.com/techcareers
   

  

 https://sebgroup.com/career
   

  

 https://bit.ly/SEBcareer

title: Data Engineer – Financial Crime Prevention | SEB, Solna
description:
*At SEB, technology is at the heart of everything we do. We are not just supporting the business—we are driving it forward with cutting-edge solutions. Cloud-native platforms and AI-driven innovation, we use a diverse tech stack to solve complex challenges for cloud-native platforms and AI-driven innovation. Join us in shaping the future of financial crime prevention by building reliable services and data pipelines that help protect society. Are you ready for the challenge?*
**Join Our Mission Against Financial Crime**
 Would you like to work with a team of highly skilled Software Engineers to build solutions that empower complex investigations? We’re looking for an experienced
 **Data Engineer** 
 with strong
 **Java** 
 skills to join our Financial Crime Prevention team—where backend engineering, data, and purpose align to detect and prevent bad actors.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As a Data Engineer in the Financial Crime Prevention team, you will build and maintain Java-based services and data processing capabilities that enable us to detect suspicious behavior and support investigations. You will work end-to-end—from design and implementation to testing, deployment, and operations—in close collaboration with engineers, analysts, and stakeholders in a highly collaborative environment.
   

  

**To thrive in this role, we believe you have:**
* Experience building data pipelines (streaming and/or batch) and working with large-scale datasets
* Solid experience with Java (e.g., Java 11+) and building backend services (Spring/Spring Boot or similar)
* Hands-on with streaming/messaging (e.g., PubSub) and event-driven architectures
* Experience with batch processing frameworks (e.g., Beam) and data modelling
* Familiarity with CI/CD, automated testing, and observability for production systems
* Experience with Infrastructure as Code (e.g., Terraform) and working in cloud environments
* Nice to have: schema registry experience and/or graph databases
* Analytical mindset and ability to solve complex technical problems
* Curiosity and a drive for continuous learning and professional growth
* A collaborative team player who enjoys sharing knowledge and learning from others


**What do we** 
**offer:**
* Meaningful work with real societal impact—helping detect and prevent financial crime
* Opportunity to explore agentic AI in a governed way (LLM-based assistants/agents using tool-calling) to improve engineering workflows and investigation support
* A chance to work with scalable data and integration platforms (streaming and batch) that are critical to SEB’s financial crime prevention capabilities
* A team culture striving for short feedback loops to be more efficient using practices such as CI/CD, automated testing, and continuous improvement
* Flexible work setup: hybrid possibilities and a sustainable work-life balance
* Opportunities to grow through learning, coaching, and working alongside experienced engineers and domain experts


**Do you want to be part of SEB?**
 Welcome to our inclusive culture, where our shared values inspire and uplift our team. We celebrate diversity and strive to ensure every employee feels seen, heard, and valued.
   

  

 Since we select candidates continuously, feel free to send in your application today, but no later than
 **2026-06-19** 
 .
   

  

**Learn more about working at SEB** 
**here**

 It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity is crucial for our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe or where they come from.

title: Security Operations Engineer
description:
**About Kambi** 




*Kambi Group plc is a leading B2B provider of premium sports betting services to licensed gaming operators. Our services provide an end-to-end solution for operators wanting to launch a standalone Sportsbook or bolster their existing offering with an innovative sports betting product. From front-end user interface to customer intelligence, risk management and odds compiling, all built on our in-house developed software, we strive to deliver the ultimate service and solution to our partners.* 




*Our vision is to create the world’s leading sports betting experiences, together with our partners.* 




**Security Operations Engineer** 




 We are now hiring for a Security Operations Engineer to join our Security team. This team drives the security programs within the business, with a heavy focus on people, processes, and automation of services. Kambi is looking for someone who wants to encourage and foster a culture of security awareness and regulatory compliance by making it easy for those less knowledgeable to Do It Right.
 



**About the Security team:** 




 You would be implementing technologies for vulnerability management, authentication and identity management, and creating security with automation. A focus for our team is to create paved roads for Development and Operations teams, to ensure they can easily secure their applications and maintain security throughout the organization. The Security Operations team also responds to incoming security threats and incidents. We provides expert security knowledge to the wider organisation. We use automation tools like Puppet, Ansible and Terraform, and work in a diverse environment with physical and virtual machines, as well as containerized workloads both on-prem and in AWS.
 



**About you:** 




 We will be very happy if you have commercial industry experience working in a security team, in either offensive or defensive roles. Additionally, previous experience working with Linux or AWS is needed, and it’s a huge bonus if you have spent time working with actively with security in these areas. Prior experience with identity and authentication systems for web and Linux integrations are valued.
 



 We'd love if you were investigative, curious and passionate about security and privacy.
 



**What’s in it for you?** 



* Help to secure people, every day
* Opportunity to work in a successful and fast-growing company
* A fun, flexible and Agile working culture
* Ability to directly influence your and your teams’ work environment and processes
* International work environment
* Opportunities to travel



*Security Engineer I Security Operations Engineer I Cyber Security Engineer I DevSecOps Engineer I Security Consultant* 
  
 


 If you're excited about this opportunity and ready to make an impact, we’d love to hear from you—apply via our careers page.
 
 #wearekambi
 



***Kambi's ongoing commitment to Diversity and Inclusion in the workplace***




*If you require any reasonable adjustment during the recruitment process, please notify your recruiter, who will assist you however they can. Diversity and inclusion is at the heart of who we are and who we aim to be. While we are proud of the positive and inclusive company culture we have created, we know we can do so much more. Kambi constantly evolves its Diversity and Inclusion strategy to ensure it becomes an even more inclusive and positive place to work, with the core management team reaffirming its commitment to delivering on employee feedback.* 




***Creating an inclusive environment***




*We believe Kambi's greatest strength is the collective talent of our employees. Kambi is committed to ensuring we create an inclusive work environment where everyone can feel valued, thrive and achieve their potential, regardless of who they are or what their background is. We know that it is only by having a balance of different voices, values and opinions that Kambi is able to be the market leader it is today.* 




*#wearekambi*
